selected_topic: Astronomers detect sugar in interstellar space
news_angle: Scientists discovered the sugar erythrulose, also found in raspberries, floating in the space between stars, revealing new chemistry in interstellar dust clouds.
why_this_is_news: This finding adds to our understanding of complex organic molecules in space and their possible roles in cosmic chemistry and origins of life.
scene_metaphor: Hunk Mao as a cool space explorer cat detecting a glowing cosmic sugar crystal in a vibrant nebula, surrounded by stars and interstellar dust.
image_prompt: an athletic orange tabby cat with strong tabby markings, wearing a sleek black hoodie and space explorer gear, floating in a colorful interstellar nebula filled with stars and swirling cosmic dust, holding a glowing crystalline sugar molecule, dynamic pose with a mischievous confident grin and large expressive eyes, cinematic anime style, vibrant sci-fi colors, energetic and youthful vibe
easter_eggs: tiny raspberry-shaped constellation in nebula background
small asteroid shaped like a cat paw
Hunk Mao's black hoodie has a small star emblem
caption: Scientists have detected the sugar erythrulose drifting between stars in deep space. Hunk Mao knows now that even the cosmos loves a little sweetness!
